info.name: FlowERP API
info.description:
Mini ERP + CRM Operations Portal for a wholesale/distribution company — REST API covering auth, customers, products, stock movements, sales challans, and purchase orders. See specs/FLO-011 through FLO-017 in the repo for the module-by-module design decisions behind each endpoint.

## Getting started

1. Import both this collection and `postman_environment.json`, and select the "FlowERP Local" environment.
2. Make sure the backend is running (`npm run dev -w backend`, or `docker compose up`) and reachable at the environment's `baseUrl` (defaults to `http://localhost:4000`).
3. Run **Auth > Login — Admin** first. Its test script saves the returned JWT into the `token` variable, which every other request in this collection uses as its Bearer auth by default.
4. Everything else can be run individually, or top-to-bottom per folder via the Collection Runner — later requests in each folder reference IDs saved by earlier ones (e.g. "Create Customer" saves `customerId`, which "Get Customer by ID" then reads).

## Roles

Four seeded demo users, all sharing the password in the `demoPassword` variable (`FlowERP123!` by default — override it if you changed `SEED_USER_PASSWORD`):

| Role | Email |
|---|---|
| ADMIN | admin@flowerp.test |
| SALES | sales@flowerp.test |
| WAREHOUSE | warehouse@flowerp.test |
| ACCOUNTS | accounts@flowerp.test |

Read access is open to all four roles on every resource. Write access is split by module: Customers = ADMIN/SALES, Products & Stock Movements = ADMIN/WAREHOUSE, Sales Challans = ADMIN/SALES, Purchase Orders = ADMIN/WAREHOUSE. ACCOUNTS is read-only everywhere. The `Auth` folder logs in as all four up front so the 403 Forbidden examples throughout this collection can use the specific wrong-role token for each endpoint.

## Response shape

Every successful response is wrapped as `{ "data": ... }` (list endpoints add `"meta": { "pagination": {...} }`); every error is `{ "error": { "code": string, "message": string, "details"?: ... } }`. See each request's saved example responses for the exact shape per endpoint, including the two 409 cases whose `details` carries a structured breakdown: confirming a Sales Challan with insufficient stock, and cancelling a Purchase Order that would take stock negative.

request.description: Public, unauthenticated liveness check. Useful to confirm `baseUrl` is correct before running anything else.

originalRequest.description: Public, unauthenticated liveness check. Useful to confirm `baseUrl` is correct before running anything else.

item.name: Auth

request.description: Logs in as the seeded ADMIN demo user. Saves the returned JWT into `adminToken` and the generic `token` variable used as the default Bearer auth for every other request in this collection — run this first.

originalRequest.description: Logs in as the seeded ADMIN demo user. Saves the returned JWT into `adminToken` and the generic `token` variable used as the default Bearer auth for every other request in this collection — run this first.

request.description: Logs in as the seeded SALES demo user. Saves the JWT into `salesToken`, used by the Sales Challan write examples and by 403 demos on Warehouse/Admin-only endpoints.

originalRequest.description: Logs in as the seeded SALES demo user. Saves the JWT into `salesToken`, used by the Sales Challan write examples and by 403 demos on Warehouse/Admin-only endpoints.

request.description: Logs in as the seeded WAREHOUSE demo user. Saves the JWT into `warehouseToken`, used for Product/Stock-Movement/Purchase-Order write examples and 403 demos on Customer/Challan endpoints.

originalRequest.description: Logs in as the seeded WAREHOUSE demo user. Saves the JWT into `warehouseToken`, used for Product/Stock-Movement/Purchase-Order write examples and 403 demos on Customer/Challan endpoints.

request.description: Logs in as the seeded ACCOUNTS demo user. ACCOUNTS has read-only access everywhere — saves the JWT into `accountsToken`, used to demonstrate 403 on write endpoints.

originalRequest.description: Logs in as the seeded ACCOUNTS demo user. ACCOUNTS has read-only access everywhere — saves the JWT into `accountsToken`, used to demonstrate 403 on write endpoints.

request.description: Same error for an unknown email or a correct email with the wrong password — the API deliberately doesn't reveal which one was wrong.

originalRequest.description: Same error for an unknown email or a correct email with the wrong password — the API deliberately doesn't reveal which one was wrong.

request.description: Returns the profile of whichever user the current Bearer token belongs to.

originalRequest.description: Returns the profile of whichever user the current Bearer token belongs to.

request.description: No Authorization header at all — every route except /health and /auth/login requires one.

originalRequest.description: No Authorization header at all — every route except /health and /auth/login requires one.

item.description: Log in as each of the four seeded roles first (shared password: {{demoPassword}}). `token` always holds whichever role logged in most recently and is the default Bearer auth below; role-specific variables (adminToken/salesToken/warehouseToken/accountsToken) are used explicitly by the 403 Forbidden examples.

item.name: Customers

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Ravi Kumar",
  "mobile": "9876543210",
  "email": "ravi@acmetraders.in",
  "businessName": "Acme Traders",
  "gstNumber": "29ABCDE1234F1Z5",
  "type": "WHOLESALE",
  "address": "12 MG Road, Bengaluru",
  "status": "ACTIVE",
  "followUpDate": "2026-08-01",
  "notes": "Prefers WhatsApp for order confirmations."
}

request.description: ADMIN and SALES only. email/businessName/gstNumber/address/notes/followUpDate are all optional; status defaults to LEAD if omitted.

response.name: 201 Created

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Ravi Kumar",
  "mobile": "9876543210",

{
  "data": {
    "id": "clcust0000000000001",
    "name": "Ravi Kumar",
    "mobile": "9876543210",
    "email": "ravi@acmetraders.in",
    "businessName": "Acme Traders",

    "updatedAt": "2026-07-22T10:00:00.000Z"

}

request.description: `name` is required (min length 1) and `type` is a required enum — both are missing/invalid here.

originalRequest.description: `name` is required (min length 1) and `type` is a required enum — both are missing/invalid here.

request.description: Customers are owned by ADMIN/SALES — a Warehouse (or Accounts) token gets 403 even with an otherwise-valid body.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

originalRequest.description: Customers are owned by ADMIN/SALES — a Warehouse (or Accounts) token gets 403 even with an otherwise-valid body.

request.description: All query params are optional. `search` matches name/mobile/email/businessName (case-insensitive). Any authenticated role can list.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description: All query params are optional. `search` matches name/mobile/email/businessName (case-insensitive). Any authenticated role can list.

request.description: Only this single-resource endpoint includes `followUps` (newest first) — the list endpoint doesn't.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description: Only this single-resource endpoint includes `followUps` (newest first) — the list endpoint doesn't.

item.name: Get Customer by ID — Not Found

request.description: Every field is optional (partial update) — send only what changed. ADMIN and SALES only.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description: Every field is optional (partial update) — send only what changed. ADMIN and SALES only.

request.description: `followUpDate` is optional; when supplied it also updates the customer's own `followUpDate` field. ADMIN and SALES only.

originalRequest.description: `followUpDate` is optional; when supplied it also updates the customer's own `followUpDate` field. ADMIN and SALES only.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

item.description: Customer CRM. Read is open to every authenticated role; create/update/follow-ups are ADMIN + SALES only.

item.name: Products

request.description: ADMIN and WAREHOUSE only. `sku` must be unique. `currentStock` is never accepted here — it starts at 0 and only changes via stock movements. The SKU is generated fresh each run (see the pre-request script) so this whole collection can be re-run against the same database without hitting a duplicate-SKU conflict.

originalRequest.description: ADMIN and WAREHOUSE only. `sku` must be unique. `currentStock` is never accepted here — it starts at 0 and only changes via stock movements. The SKU is generated fresh each run (see the pre-request script) so this whole collection can be re-run against the same database without hitting a duplicate-SKU conflict.

request.description: A second product, used later by the multi-line-item Sales Challan and Purchase Order examples. SKU generated fresh each run, same reasoning as "Create Product".

originalRequest.description: A second product, used later by the multi-line-item Sales Challan and Purchase Order examples. SKU generated fresh each run, same reasoning as "Create Product".

request.description: Reuses the same `{{productSku}}` value "Create Product" above just created — `sku` has a unique constraint in the database.

response.name: 409 Conflict

originalRequest.description: Reuses the same `{{productSku}}` value "Create Product" above just created — `sku` has a unique constraint in the database.

request.description: The product catalog is owned by ADMIN/WAREHOUSE — a Sales (or Accounts) token gets 403.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

originalRequest.description: The product catalog is owned by ADMIN/WAREHOUSE — a Sales (or Accounts) token gets 403.

request.description: `search` matches name/SKU (case-insensitive); `category` is a case-insensitive contains-match.

originalRequest.description: `search` matches name/SKU (case-insensitive); `category` is a case-insensitive contains-match.

request.description: Filters to products where `currentStock <= minStockAlertQuantity` — `isLowStock` on each returned product is derived the same way, never stored.

originalRequest.description: Filters to products where `currentStock <= minStockAlertQuantity` — `isLowStock` on each returned product is derived the same way, never stored.

item.name: Get Product by ID — Not Found

request.description: Every field is optional. `sku` and `currentStock` are deliberately not editable here — SKU is immutable and stock only moves through the stock-movement ledger. ADMIN and WAREHOUSE only.

originalRequest.description: Every field is optional. `sku` and `currentStock` are deliberately not editable here — SKU is immutable and stock only moves through the stock-movement ledger. ADMIN and WAREHOUSE only.

item.description: Product & inventory catalog. Read is open to every authenticated role; create/update are ADMIN + WAREHOUSE only. Stock levels themselves are managed under Stock Movements, not here.

item.name: Stock Movements

request.description: ADMIN and WAREHOUSE only. `sourceType` is always forced to MANUAL here (CHALLAN/PURCHASE_ORDER movements are only ever created internally by those flows).

originalRequest.description: ADMIN and WAREHOUSE only. `sourceType` is always forced to MANUAL here (CHALLAN/PURCHASE_ORDER movements are only ever created internally by those flows).

request.description: Stocks up the second product too, so the multi-item Sales Challan / Purchase Order examples further down have enough stock to work with.

originalRequest.description: Stocks up the second product too, so the multi-item Sales Challan / Purchase Order examples further down have enough stock to work with.

request.description: A manual correction outward — e.g. damaged/written-off stock, independent of any challan.

originalRequest.description: A manual correction outward — e.g. damaged/written-off stock, independent of any challan.

request.description: An OUT movement is rejected atomically (a guarded UPDATE, not a check-then-write) whenever it would take `currentStock` negative — the core invariant behind FLO-014's stock ledger.

originalRequest.description: An OUT movement is rejected atomically (a guarded UPDATE, not a check-then-write) whenever it would take `currentStock` negative — the core invariant behind FLO-014's stock ledger.

item.name: List Stock Movements for a Product

request.description: The full audit trail for one product, newest first. Only `page`/`limit` are supported — no search/filter on this endpoint.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description: The full audit trail for one product, newest first. Only `page`/`limit` are supported — no search/filter on this endpoint.

item.name: List Stock Movements — Product Not Found

item.description: Manual stock adjustments and the per-product movement ledger (nested under /products/:id). ADMIN and WAREHOUSE can record movements; every role can read the ledger. Sales Challan confirm/cancel and Purchase Order receive/cancel create their own IN/OUT movements automatically — see those folders.

item.name: Sales Challans

request.description: ADMIN and SALES only. Auto-generates `challanNumber`, snapshots each product's name/SKU/price at this moment, and starts as DRAFT — no stock is deducted until Confirm.

originalRequest.description: ADMIN and SALES only. Auto-generates `challanNumber`, snapshots each product's name/SKU/price at this moment, and starts as DRAFT — no stock is deducted until Confirm.

request.description: `items` requires at least one line item.

response.name: 400 Validation Error

originalRequest.description: `items` requires at least one line item.

request.description: Sales Challans are owned end-to-end by ADMIN/SALES — Warehouse can only view them.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

originalRequest.description: Sales Challans are owned end-to-end by ADMIN/SALES — Warehouse can only view them.

request.description: `search` (challan number), `status`, and `customerId` are all optional filters. List items don't include `items[]` — fetch by ID for line items.

originalRequest.description: `search` (challan number), `status`, and `customerId` are all optional filters. List items don't include `items[]` — fetch by ID for line items.

item.name: Get Challan by ID — Not Found

request.description: Only a DRAFT challan can be edited. `items`, when supplied, fully REPLACES the existing line items (not a diff/merge) — always send the complete new set.

originalRequest.description: Only a DRAFT challan can be edited. `items`, when supplied, fully REPLACES the existing line items (not a diff/merge) — always send the complete new set.

request.description: Transactionally deducts stock (one OUT movement per line item, sourceType CHALLAN) and moves the challan to CONFIRMED. All-or-nothing: if any line item lacks sufficient stock, nothing is deducted (see the Insufficient Stock example below).

originalRequest.description: Transactionally deducts stock (one OUT movement per line item, sourceType CHALLAN) and moves the challan to CONFIRMED. All-or-nothing: if any line item lacks sufficient stock, nothing is deducted (see the Insufficient Stock example below).

request.description: Re-running Confirm on the now-CONFIRMED challan from the previous request.

response.name: 409 Conflict

originalRequest.description: Re-running Confirm on the now-CONFIRMED challan from the previous request.

request.description: Deliberately requests far more than is currently in stock, to demonstrate the Confirm 409 below live.

originalRequest.description: Deliberately requests far more than is currently in stock, to demonstrate the Confirm 409 below live.

request.description: Confirms the deliberately-overdrawn challan above. `details` lists exactly the line items that don't have enough stock, with what was requested vs. what's actually available — nothing is deducted for any item when this happens.

originalRequest.description: Confirms the deliberately-overdrawn challan above. `details` lists exactly the line items that don't have enough stock, with what was requested vs. what's actually available — nothing is deducted for any item when this happens.

request.description: A DRAFT challan cancels with no stock effect. Cancelling a CONFIRMED challan instead reverses its deduction via compensating IN movements — same endpoint either way.

originalRequest.description: A DRAFT challan cancels with no stock effect. Cancelling a CONFIRMED challan instead reverses its deduction via compensating IN movements — same endpoint either way.

request.description: Re-running Cancel on the challan just cancelled above.

originalRequest.description: Re-running Cancel on the challan just cancelled above.

item.description: Sales Challan lifecycle: Draft -> Confirm (deducts stock) -> optionally Cancel (reverses stock if it was Confirmed). Read is open to every role; all writes are ADMIN + SALES only.

item.name: Purchase Orders

request.description: ADMIN and WAREHOUSE only. There's no supplier CRM — supplier is a plain text field. Auto-generates `poNumber`, snapshots product name/SKU, starts DRAFT — no stock added until Receive.

originalRequest.description: ADMIN and WAREHOUSE only. There's no supplier CRM — supplier is a plain text field. Auto-generates `poNumber`, snapshots product name/SKU, starts DRAFT — no stock added until Receive.

response.name: 400 Validation Error
404 Not Found

request.description: Purchase Orders are the inbound counterpart to Sales Challans, owned by ADMIN/WAREHOUSE — Sales can only view them.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

originalRequest.description: Purchase Orders are the inbound counterpart to Sales Challans, owned by ADMIN/WAREHOUSE — Sales can only view them.

request.description: `search` matches PO number or supplier name (case-insensitive). Totals are always present, even on the list view, since they're computed from line items rather than stored.

originalRequest.description: `search` matches PO number or supplier name (case-insensitive). Totals are always present, even on the list view, since they're computed from line items rather than stored.

item.name: Get Purchase Order by ID — Not Found

request.description: Only a DRAFT PO can be edited. `items`, when supplied, fully replaces the existing line items.

originalRequest.description: Only a DRAFT PO can be edited. `items`, when supplied, fully replaces the existing line items.

request.description: Transactionally adds stock (one IN movement per line item, sourceType PURCHASE_ORDER) and moves the PO to RECEIVED. Unlike a challan Confirm, IN movements never fail on a stock check.

originalRequest.description: Transactionally adds stock (one IN movement per line item, sourceType PURCHASE_ORDER) and moves the PO to RECEIVED. Unlike a challan Confirm, IN movements never fail on a stock check.

request.description: Re-running Receive on the now-RECEIVED PO from the previous request.

response.name: 409 Conflict

originalRequest.description: Re-running Receive on the now-RECEIVED PO from the previous request.

request.description: A DRAFT PO cancels with no stock effect. Cancelling a RECEIVED PO instead reverses its stock addition via compensating OUT movements — see the negative-stock guard demo below for when that reversal is blocked.

originalRequest.description: A DRAFT PO cancels with no stock effect. Cancelling a RECEIVED PO instead reverses its stock addition via compensating OUT movements — see the negative-stock guard demo below for when that reversal is blocked.

item.description: Purchase Order lifecycle: Draft -> Receive (adds stock) -> optionally Cancel (reverses stock if it was Received, blocked if that reversal would go negative — see "Negative-Stock Guard Demo"). Read is open to every role; all writes are ADMIN + WAREHOUSE only.

item.name: Negative-Stock Guard Demo

request.description: A product used only by this self-contained demo, so the scenario's stock math doesn't depend on anything run earlier in the collection. SKU generated fresh each run so this demo can be re-run repeatedly.

originalRequest.description: A product used only by this self-contained demo, so the scenario's stock math doesn't depend on anything run earlier in the collection. SKU generated fresh each run so this demo can be re-run repeatedly.

item.name: 4. Create Challan (qty 7 of the same product)

request.description: Sells most of that stock elsewhere, so only 3 units remain once this is confirmed — set up deliberately so the PO cancel below has too little stock to reverse against.

originalRequest.description: Sells most of that stock elsewhere, so only 3 units remain once this is confirmed — set up deliberately so the PO cancel below has too little stock to reverse against.

item.name: 6. Cancel the Purchase Order — Blocked (negative stock)

request.description: Cancelling this RECEIVED PO would reverse its +10 receipt with a -10 OUT movement — but only 3 units are left in stock (7 were sold via the challan above), so it's rejected before touching anything. This is the same guarded, all-or-nothing pattern used everywhere stock is decremented, applied to a receipt reversal instead of a sale.

response.name: 409 Conflict — Would Go Negative

originalRequest.description: Cancelling this RECEIVED PO would reverse its +10 receipt with a -10 OUT movement — but only 3 units are left in stock (7 were sold via the challan above), so it's rejected before touching anything. This is the same guarded, all-or-nothing pattern used everywhere stock is decremented, applied to a receipt reversal instead of a sale.

item.description: A deliberately self-contained, deterministic scenario (its own product/PO/challan, independent of everything else in this collection) demonstrating the one 409 case that's otherwise hard to reproduce on demand: cancelling a Received PO is blocked outright if reversing it would ever take a product's stock negative.
